items.label: s#2
items.answer: {"move":"c1a1","rationale":"Ra1+ is a forcing key: Black’s king has no flight or interposition and must capture on a1. White then plays Qb2+, again leaving Black only Qxb2. items.answer_rationale: Ra1+ is a forcing key: Black’s king has no flight or interposition and must capture on a1. White then plays Qb2+, again leaving Black only Qxb2. That capture gives check to the white king on c2, and the queen on b2 is protected by the black king on a1, so it is checkmate.

items.answer: {"moves":["a1a2","a2a8"],"rationale":"White first makes the quiet rook move Ra2, which gives no check. The second move Ra8 gives check along the eighth rank. items.answer_rationale: White first makes the quiet rook move Ra2, which gives no check. The second move Ra8 gives check along the eighth rank. Black's king on h8 cannot escape: g8 is covered by the rook, while g7 and h7 are occupied by Black's own pawns and cannot interpose or capture the rook. Thus Ra8 is checkmate.
